# Comparison Matrix

Living scoring table. One row per candidate, one column per rubric criterion.
Scores: `low` / `med` / `high` / `n/a`.

**Auto-generated by `tests/harness/build-matrix.py` from `tests/results/*/scores.json`.**
Do not edit by hand — run `scripts/run-all.sh` or `tests/harness/build-matrix.py` to regenerate.

Row order is stable once scored. Column order may change freely.

## Matrix

| Candidate | Pattern coverage | Detection rigor | Voice calibration | Modes | Convergence | Ethics framing | Skill structure | Maturity | Portability | Conciseness |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| stephenturner-skill-deslop | high | low | low | low | low | low | med | low | med | high |
| blader-humanizer | high | med | high | low | med | low | high | med | high | low |
| brandonwise-humanizer | high | high | low | med | low | low | high | med | med | med |
| conorbronsdon-avoid-ai-writing | high | high | high | high | high | high | high | med | high | low |
| lguz-humanize-writing-skill | high | low | high | low | low | low | high | low | med | high |

## Legend

- `high` — strongly meets criterion; best-in-class.
- `med` — meets criterion adequately.
- `low` — weakly meets or misses criterion.
- `n/a` — criterion does not apply.
- empty — not yet scored.

## Notes

- Scores are auto-generated by an LLM judge. See `tests/harness/` for the harness.
- Decisions citing these scores live in `docs/adr/`.
- Per-candidate verdicts and rationales: `tests/results/<short-name>/scores.json`.
